text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.

In this podcast, we help you understand 
everyday English conversations.
I'm Neil.
And I'm Georgie.
Don't forget to go to our website to find a

text version of this episode to read 
along: BBC Learning English dot com.
Hi, Georgie. How are you?

I'm great, thank you, Neil. 
I just had a delicious lunch.
Oh, nice. What did you eat for lunch?
I had pasta. What did you eat?
I had a chicken sandwich.

Mmm. Well, today's episode is 
all about restaurants and food.
Great. I love it when we talk about food.
Me too.
Neil, do you go to restaurants often?
Not that often. Maybe when I meet some friends.
How about you? Do you go to restaurants often?
No, I don't go often. Probably once a month.

OK, and when you go to a restaurant, 
what's your favourite part of eating there?
I like to be around lots of people.

I really love it when a 
restaurant has a good atmosphere.
I love spending time with friends.

What about you? Why do you 
like eating in a restaurant?

Well, I like to go to a restaurant that 
serves food that I don't know how to cook.
Yep, that's a good point.

So, restaurants with food from 
countries that I don’t know very well

or that are very different types 
of food to the food I make.

It’s really nice to eat out and 
that means to eat not at your home

because you don't have to make the 
food and you don't have to wash up.
That's true, yes.

It’s also nice to eat out for a special 
occasion. So, like a birthday or a celebration.

Yes and another special occasion could 
be something like Christmas and we at

BBC Learning English are eating 
out for that special occasion.
We're having a celebration, aren't we?

Yes, every year we have a Christmas party 
and we usually eat out in a restaurant.

So, Neil, you said you like eating 
out in lots of different types of
restaurants. What do you usually order?
Well, I really like spicy food like Indian curries and I like fish. So I like to get fish curry.

Mmm that sounds delicious! And we have 
a special way of ordering don't we?

What are some phrases we can use 
to order things in restaurants?
Yeah, so we can say: 'I'll have the fish curry.'
'I'll have the fish curry, please.'

Yes, and you can also say, 'Can I have 
the fish curry, please?', as well.

OK, Georgie, what do you 
usually order in a restaurant?
I also usually get the fish. Or a risotto.
OK, so let's say we are in a restaurant now. I'm

the waiter. I say to you: 
'Are you ready to order?'
'Yes, I am.'
'And what would you like?'
'Can I get the risotto, please?'
'Yes, of course.'
So we can also say, 'Can I get the risotto?',
as well as can I have the risotto. And notice that we say 'the risotto', not a risotto.
That's right.

Let's recap the language we 
learned during the conversation.

We had 'eat out' which means 'eat a meal 
at a restaurant rather than at home'.

'Special occasion' – 'a birthday, 
anniversary, or other reason to celebrate'.

And we learned that when we order 
in a restaurant, we usually say:
'I'll have the something… fish… chicken… risotto.'

Or, 'Can I get the...', 
and what you want to order.
So, for example, when I eat at my favourite restaurant, I always get 'the risotto'.
Thanks for listening to Real Easy English.
If you like restaurants, you could try our drama

series The White Elephant all 
about a busy restaurant kitchen.
Bye.
